original_description: Fetches annual and quarterly reports (10-K, 10-Q, 20-F) from SEC EDGAR using stock tickers.
best_description: Fetches annual and quarterly reports (10-K, 10-Q, 20-F) from SEC EDGAR using stock tickers.
final_description: The direct pipeline to SEC EDGAR for reading what companies officially disclosed in regulatory filings. Invoke when any research task requires the actual text of a company's own annual or quarterly report — 10-K (US annual), 20-F (foreign annual), 10-Q (quarterly). Use for competitive teardowns, investment research, and risk analysis that must cite primary source disclosures: risk factors, MD&A, capex commitments, export control language, segment descriptions, regulatory exposure. When a user wants to know what a company *said in its filing* — not what news reported, not what analysts summarized — this is the skill. Works via ticker for any public company. Does not cover earnings call transcripts, investor day presentations, or private companies.

history.description: Fetches annual and quarterly reports (10-K, 10-Q, 20-F) from SEC EDGAR using stock tickers.

train_results.query: i want to compare how amazon and google describe their AI infrastructure capex in their most recent annual filings. pull both and put them side by side
for the coinbase competitive brief i'm putting together — can you get their latest 10-K and summarize how they frame regulatory risk in the risk factors section
i need to understand how nvidia frames the export control risks in their latest annual report — specifically anything about china restrictions on H100s
im looking at spotify as part of a music streaming competitive analysis. can you grab their latest annual filing? they're listed on nyse but incorporated in luxembourg so they might file a 20-F
i'm building out a competitive teardown of the hyperscaler market — can you pull the latest 10-K for MSFT and find what they say about azure growth and capex commitments
doing some research on semi equipment suppliers. find trends from asml's latest SEC filings — specifically want to know how they're framing demand from AI customers. ticker is ASML
pull the slide deck from microsoft's last investor day — i want to see their long-range azure growth projections
can you pull the transcript from nvidia's last earnings call? want to see what jensen said about data center demand
find recent news about apple's antitrust cases in the EU — want to understand the regulatory landscape before the earnings call
i'm researching anthropic's business model and revenue structure. what do we know about how they make money?
i think apple's revenue last year was around $390B and net margin around 26%. can you confirm that and sanity check it?
stripe just announced they're going public. what do we know about their revenue and growth trajectory from the press coverage?

test_results.query: what were duolingo's operating expenses and R&D spend in their last quarterly filing? trying to understand their cost structure
pull the Q2 10-Q for salesforce (CRM) and look for their remaining performance obligations number — want to compare it to last quarter
what does palantir say about concentration risk in their most recent annual report? i remember they have some chunky government contracts
working on a bear case for TSLA. pull the risk factors from their most recent 10-K — specifically looking for anything about autonomous driving liability or regulatory exposure
what are sell-side analysts saying about tesla's autonomous driving timeline? looking for consensus estimates and price targets
what's microsoft's current pe ratio and how does it compare to google's? just need a quick sanity check on valuation
use perplexity to look into who the main competitors are for palantir in the government analytics space
can you explain the difference between a 10-K and an S-1? asking because a friend's company is going through an IPO process

history.description: Use when the user wants to read, analyze, or extract information from SEC filings — annual reports (10-K, 20-F) or quarterly reports (10-Q) — for any publicly traded company. Trigger on requests like: pulling a company's latest annual filing, reading risk factors or MD&A sections, comparing how two companies describe a topic in their filings, researching regulatory risk or capital expenditure disclosures, or doing competitive analysis using official SEC documents. Works with stock tickers (AAPL, NVDA, MSFT, etc.). Use this when the user's research question can be answered from a company's own regulatory filings rather than news, earnings calls, or general web search.

history.description: Use this skill whenever the user wants to fetch or read from a company's actual SEC filings: 10-K annual reports, 10-Q quarterly reports, or 20-F foreign annual reports. Trigger when the user asks to pull, read, or extract content from official regulatory filings — risk factors, MD&A, capex disclosures, business descriptions, or any named section of an annual or quarterly report. Also use for competitive analysis that requires reading what companies wrote in their own filings. Works via stock ticker for any publicly traded company. Do NOT use for earnings call transcripts, investor day decks, news coverage, or research on private companies.

history.description: ALWAYS use this skill when a user wants to read what a company wrote in an annual or quarterly report — 10-K, 10-Q, or 20-F. This is the direct pipeline to SEC EDGAR: it fetches the actual filing text, not news or summaries. Trigger on any request to pull, read, or extract from a company's own regulatory filing: risk factors, MD&A, capex language, business descriptions, competitive positioning. Use for competitive research that requires reading the primary source — what a company disclosed in its own filing. Works for any publicly traded company via ticker. Do NOT use for earnings call transcripts, investor presentations, news articles, or research on private companies.
